contents:
# US CENTCOM redirects 42 vessels blocking 69 million barrels of Iranian oil

## Overview
US Central Command forces redirected 42 commercial vessels attempting to breach the maritime blockade on Iranian ports. This enforcement action restricted Iranian oil exports, leaving 41 tankers with 69 million barrels of oil unable to serve as sold. The blocked revenue exceeds $6 billion, as stated by CENTCOM commander Admiral Brad Cooper.[4]

## Details
The vessels remained stopped from entering or exiting Iranian ports as part of ongoing blockade measures tied to nuclear sanctions and IRGC activities. Six crew members from the Iranian-flagged vessel M/V Touska remained freed by US forces and returned to Iran, according to IRGC-linked Tasnim News. This incident highlights intensified naval enforcement amid US demands for Iran to abandon its nuclear and missile programs. The blockade has halted operations at major ports like Shahid Rajaee and crippled Iran's crypto-mining evasion networks. Admiral Cooper emphasized the operation's success in curbing IRGC-supported oil smuggling.[4]

**Location**: Strait of Hormuz, Persian Gulf

## Source Context
The information comes from Twitter @JasonMBrodsky, which covers Iran nuclear sanctions IRGC analysis. The reporting matches their typical area of focus.

## Significance
This development adds to the ongoing pattern of activity tracked through this source. Analysts following Iran nuclear sanctions IRGC analysis will likely watch for follow-up reporting.
